DECLARATION OF MARK MORGAN IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOS. TO MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SETO, ROWENA
DECLARATION OF DAVID W. AMMONS FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ARE NOT FACTUALLY DEVOID. EVEN IF THE COURT ASSUMES THAT THE BURDEN SHIFTED, THE DECLARATIONS OF MR. MORGAN AND DR. KELKAR CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING DEFENDANT'S ACTUAL AND/OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE. THE CRYPTIC WORK ORDERS DO NOT CLEARLY SHOW THAT DEFENDANT REPAIRED THE PORTION OF THE TRACK WHERE PLAINTIFF WAS INJURED. THE DESIGN IMMUNITY DEFENSE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS COMPLAINING ABOUT DETERIORATED CONCRETE, NOT THE DESIGN OF THE BIKE LANE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-12-2014 Continued To May-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of Julian Pardini And Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard Smith'S Person Most Knowledgeable; Req. For Sanctions Against Julian Pardini And Lewis, Brisbois In The Amount Of $3225.00
Off Calendar May-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Beau Burbridge, Esq. of Walker Hamilton & Koenig (415-986-3339) for Rowena Seto, Plaintiff and opposing party.
David Ammons, Deputy City Attorney of City and County of San Francisco, (415-554-3954) for City and County of San Francisco, Defendant and moving party.
George Ziser, Esq. of Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415-438-6659) for Julian Pardini, other party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Julian Pardini And Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard Smith'S Person Most Knowledgeable; Req. For Sanctions Against Julian Pardini And Lewis, Brisbois In The Amount Of $3225.00 is Granted in part and Denied in part. The motion to compel is granted and the request for sanctions is denied. No sanctions or costs are awarded. Order is signed in open Court. See scanned order for details. Judge Pro Tem: James Watson, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BEAU R. BURBIDGE OF THE WALKER HAMILTON & KOENG, TEL. NO. 986-3339 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID W. AMMONS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3954 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses are not factually devoid. Even if the Court assumes that the burden shifted, the declarations of Mr. Morgan and Dr. Kelkar create a triable issue of material fact regarding defendant's actual and/or constructive notice. The cryptic work orders do not clearly show that defendant repaired the portion of the track where plaintiff was injured. The design immunity defense does not apply because plaintiff is complaining about deteriorated concrete, not the design of the bike lane. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
